channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Gunman shoots several tourists at historic pyramids in Mexico
item.description: A man standing atop one of the historic Teotihuacan pyramids opened fire on tourists Monday, killing one Canadian and leaving at least 13 people, authorities said.
item.content:encoded:
A man standing atop one of the historic Teotihuacan pyramids opened fire on tourists Monday, killing one Canadian and leaving at least 13 people, authorities said.

(Image credit: Eduardo Verdugo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Cuba confirms meeting with US officials on island, wants energy blockade lifted
item.description: Cuba's government confirmed that it had recently met with U.S. officials on the island as tensions between the two sides remain high over the U.S. energy blockade of the Caribbean country.
item.content:encoded:
Cuba's government confirmed that it had recently met with U.S. officials on the island as tensions between the two sides remain high over the U.S. energy blockade of the Caribbean country.

(Image credit: Ramon Espinosa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Japan approves scrapping a ban on lethal weapons exports
item.description: The approval clears a final set of hurdles for Japan's postwar arms sales and facilitate its future sale of weapons such as a next-generation fighter jet and combat drones.
item.content:encoded:
The approval clears a final set of hurdles for Japan's postwar arms sales and facilitate its future sale of weapons such as a next-generation fighter jet and combat drones.

(Image credit: Keisuke Hosojima/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Canada's PM Carney indicates a move away from economic reliance on the U.S.
item.description: Canada's close economic ties with the U.S. are now a "weakness," not a strength, Prime Minister Mark Carney says. In a video address to the nation, he outlines plans to expand trade beyond America.
item.content:encoded: Canada's close economic ties with the U.S. are now a "weakness," not a strength, Prime Minister Mark Carney says. In a video address to the nation, he outlines plans to expand trade beyond America.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: U.S.-Iran talks are uncertain as ceasefire is set to expire
item.description: As the clock ticks down on the ceasefire, Iran says it may not attend fresh talks with the U.S. That's as a temporary ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon appears to be holding.
item.content:encoded: As the clock ticks down on the ceasefire, Iran says it may not attend fresh talks with the U.S. That's as a temporary ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon appears to be holding.
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: Peace talks are in doubt as the U.S. seizes an Iranian ship
item.description: President Trump said a U.S. delegation will head to Pakistan to resume talks to end the war with Iran, but Tehran expressed reluctance after the U.S. seized one of its cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump said a U.S. delegation will head to Pakistan to resume talks to end the war with Iran, but Tehran expressed reluctance after the U.S. seized one of its cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship. And, tariff refund portal launches
item.description: U.S. forces seized an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. And, an online government portal for processing tariff refunds launches today.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. forces seized an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. And, an online government portal for processing tariff refunds launches today.

(Image credit: Asghar Besharati)
item.dc:creator: Suzanne Nuyen

item.title: After massive cuts, U.S. says HIV work abroad is going well, but experts disagree
item.description: The Trump administration has released new data on the country's HIV/AIDS work abroad. It is claiming the numbers are good news, but many HIV experts say they paint a worrisome picture.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration has released new data on the country's HIV/AIDS work abroad. It is claiming the numbers are good news, but many HIV experts say they paint a worrisome picture.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Ukraine's fast-growing defense tech sector takes center stage in Iran war
item.description: While Ukraine seeks to shift its image from not only a recipient of military aid but also a provider, its battle-tested, anti-drone tech is taking center stage in the Iran war.
item.content:encoded: While Ukraine seeks to shift its image from not only a recipient of military aid but also a provider, its battle-tested, anti-drone tech is taking center stage in the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: A humanoid robot sprints past the human half-marathon world record in Beijing race
item.description: The winner from Honor, a Chinese smartphone maker, completed the race in 50 minutes and 26 seconds, running faster than the human world record in a show of China's technological leaps.
item.content:encoded:
The winner from Honor, a Chinese smartphone maker, completed the race in 50 minutes and 26 seconds, running faster than the human world record in a show of China's technological leaps.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
